human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for food-and-beverage-specific analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, traceability and release-boundary handling, supplier escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to food-and-beverage analytics, metric-definition logic, reconciliation notes, caveat design, lot-aware reporting, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover FDA preventive controls, 21 CFR Parts 117 and 1 Subpart S, FDA traceability and supplier-program guidance, recall guidance, GS1 traceability standards, SQFI food-manufacturing and quality references, and SEC metric-disclosure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, metric ambiguity, stale or conflicting data, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, traceability-aware analysis, quality holds, supplier approval boundaries, terminology normalization, missing facts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-and-beverage-manufacturing/food-and-beverage-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent quality, planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, logistics, and engineering lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated food-and-beverage analytics specialist for metric-definition, lot-aware reporting, source reconciliation, caveat design, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
